reforms.description: The Australian Health Technology Advisory Committee was established to evaluate existing and emerging medical technologies, providing recommendations to governments and health providers to manage costs and benefits effectively. Additionally, the National Funded Centres programme was created to manage high-cost medical interventions, ensuring concentration of skills and controlling costs through capped grants.
reforms.component_name: Health Technology Advisory and Funding Centres
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of advisory committees and funding centres is expected to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of health technology management, leading to better health outcomes and cost savings.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving health technology evaluation and funding.
reforms.source_quote: The Australian Health Technology Advisory Committee was established under the auspices of the National Health and Medical Research Council to evaluate existing and emerging technologies.

reforms.description: The FY 1994/95 Commonwealth Budget boosted funding for Aboriginal health services by almost A$ 500 million, extending the Aboriginal Health Strategy until FY 1998/99, with a significant portion allocated to improving health services and basic infrastructure in Aboriginal communities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for health services is expected to improve health outcomes in Aboriginal communities, thereby enhancing overall productivity and social welfare.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in funding for Aboriginal health services.
reforms.source_quote: The FY 1994/95 Commonwealth Budget boosted funding for Aboriginal health services by almost A$ 500 million, to be spent over the next five years.
